name: CHERI / Morello / CheriBSD
summary: Cambridge / SRI International's CHERI replaces 64-bit pointers with 128-bit unforgeable capabilities — address + bounds + permissions + 1-bit validity tag in tagged memory — enforced by the ISA. Hardware-enforced spatial safety and strong pointer provenance/integrity for C/C++. Morello is the ARM research prototype; CheriBSD is the reference OS.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Cambridge / SRI International's **CHERI** replaces 64-bit pointers with 128-bit unforgeable capabilities — address + bounds + permissions + 1-bit validity tag in tagged memory — enforced by the ISA. It provides hardware-enforced spatial safety and strong pointer provenance/integrity for C/C++; temporal safety requires an accompanying revocation, quarantine, or allocation discipline.

CHERI sits at the most ambitious end of the hardware-assisted memory-safety design spectrum: it changes the pointer representation itself rather than adding tags or signatures to existing pointers. The cost is doubled pointer size and ABI-level changes; the payoff is a fully bounded, provenance-tracked addressing model.

sections.title: CHERI-128 architecture
sections.content:
Capabilities are 128-bit pointers carrying a base address, bounds, permissions (load/store/execute/seal/etc.), and a 1-bit validity tag held in tagged physical memory. The ISA forbids forging or out-of-bounds increment of capabilities; any violation traps. A pure-capability ABI (CheriABI) replaces all pointer-bearing register and memory operations with capability-bearing forms.

Overhead ~2.2–3.2% geomean for SPECint 2006 in pure-cap mode on optimised microarchitectures. Doubled pointer size also imposes significant memory overhead.

sections.title: Morello prototype
sections.content:
Morello is Arm's research prototype implementing CHERI on a Neoverse-class core. Its overhead is much higher than the architectural ideal because the store buffers were sized for 64-bit pointers — capability stores burn extra cycles. Morello demonstrated that pure-capability C/C++ stacks are buildable end-to-end but not that the cost can be made negligible without microarchitectural redesign.

Status (as of 2026-04): Morello is a research prototype, not commercial; production silicon for a Morello successor is still pending.

sections.title: CheriBSD and compartmentalisation
sections.content:
CheriBSD is the reference operating system for CHERI: a FreeBSD fork that runs entirely in pure-capability mode. Software compartmentalisation (sub-library `c18n` in CheriBSD 25.03 isolating malloc/syscall surface) and the pure-capability ABI (CheriABI) build on the ISA.

Wind River joined the CHERI Alliance in April 2026 (porting VxWorks/Helix to CHERI on RISC-V). Programming-model details, including compartments, are surveyed alongside other capability programming models.
